title: Operation Ghost Proxy
brief: Infiltrate Orion Logistics staging network and exfiltrate client billing evidence without hitting max trace.

debrief.success: Clean pull. That ledger ties the manual adjustments to an internal account. Stay off-grid for 24 hours; Orion will be sweeping logs.
debrief.fail: You burned the session. Orion's SOC will rotate keys and harden paths. If you retry, go slower: lay low when alerts hit and keep trace under budget.

steps.title: Step 1: Find the edge gateway
steps.text: Run scan to list adjacent hosts, then probe <host> for a remote port sweep (open ports only). After connect, enum maps ports to exploit ids — probe does not duplicate enum.

steps.title: Step 2: Enumerate services on your rig
steps.text: On local, enum shows the staging SSH surface. You must run exploit weak-ssh here before the gateway will accept a connect — that hop is blocked until staging credentials exist.

steps.title: Step 3: Stage credentials for the gateway hop
steps.text: Run exploit weak-ssh on local (staging / trust material). This is not the same as owning the edge box yet — it only authorizes the link.

steps.title: Step 4: Pivot to the edge gateway
steps.text: Now connect gw-edge. The SSH theater runs only after staging succeeded.

steps.title: Step 5: Own the gateway
steps.text: On gw-edge, enum again — the weak-ssh entry here is the real compromise. Exploit it to own the node and read files.

steps.title: Step 6: Pivot to the app tier
steps.text: Probe app-api from the graph (remote sweep: ports only). You cannot connect app-api until the edge gateway is owned — exploit weak-ssh on gw-edge first; then connect app-api.

steps.title: Step 7: Compromise the app
steps.text: After gw-edge is owned, connect app-api, enumerate, then exploit.

steps.title: Step 8: Find the database core
steps.text: The objective file lives on db-core—sweep its ports, then pivot.

steps.title: Step 9: Root the target
steps.text: Exploit the database misconfiguration, then exfiltrate the target file.

steps.title: Step 10: Exfiltrate evidence
steps.text: Exfiltration is noisy. If SOC is active, consider laylow first.

steps.title: Step 11: Submit
steps.text: Once exfil is complete, submit the objective to close the operation.

services.name: ssh
services.vulnRef: Staging SSH client — establish operator trust material before the edge gateway accepts your session (fiction; not a second compromise of local)

services.name: ssh
services.vulnRef: CVE-2024-6387 (OpenSSH regreSSHion) — unauthenticated RCE class on affected versions

files.content: Failed key attempts from app-api. Maintenance account still enabled.
Reminder: rotate the app maintenance artifact after the quarter close.

artifact.description: A fictional maintenance credential artifact that can unlock alternate access paths to app-api in this game.

services.name: http
services.vulnRef: SSTI / OGNL-style template injection (cf. CVE-2022-26134 class — server-side template RCE)

services.name: ssh
services.vulnRef: SSH auth surface — alternate path when maintenance key artifact is available (no CVE; access gating)

files.content: stacktrace: query endpoint retries against db-core failed at 00:16

INTERNAL — ticket search API builds SQL with string concat (title = '" + user + "'). Red-team note: try sql demo / sql translate in the wargame to see how your input maps to psql. Fix = prepared statements + least privilege DB role.

services.name: postgres
services.vulnRef: CVE-2019-9193 (PostgreSQL COPY FROM PROGRAM) — dangerous COPY / superuser misconfig class

files.content: Do not expose manual adjustments before quarter close.
